_purpose: Canonical AgentRemote task board. Root tasks.json is only a compatibility pointer.
rules: Stay inside /Users/richardadair/ai_projects/agent-launch-scripts unless Richard explicitly authorizes another repo.
Do not mutate Richard's live iTerm/tmux/AgentRemote desktop unless he explicitly asks for that exact live action in the current turn.
Use this file as the single local board; do not add a second divergent task list at root tasks.json.
Every feature must have an owner doc, expected behavior, regression tests or an explicit manual verification path.
Do not mark live AgentRemote behavior done from static tests alone.

tasks.title: Create complete AgentRemote feature inventory
acceptance: Every visible AgentRemote feature and launcher/runtime feature has a named entry.
Each entry includes source files, expected behavior, tests, and live-safe verification notes.
Inventory covers spawn, attach, stop/restart, layout, model/runtime picker, startup injection, paste, keyboard shortcuts, pet chat, voice, registry, sidecar, hooks, and cleanup.

PRD links to the operator contract, design system, launch docs, feature index, and quality gates.
Future sessions can read the PRD and know what must not regress.

Matrix distinguishes static tests, isolated tmux tests, and live-desktop checks that require Richard's approval.
Known regressions from this session are included: image paste, Option key word navigation, Claude Enter injection, model picker, invisible viewer attach, pane/window naming.

Existing Codex registry entries use reasoning_effort xhigh.
Focused harness/launcher tests pass.

tasks.title: Fix simultaneous timed Enter without breaking paste or Option+Backspace
tasks.description: Live symptom is not a generic stray Enter: a simultaneous/timed Enter is being sent into panes. Prior attempted guard suppressed the symptom by breaking expected image paste and Option+Backspace word deletion. Diagnose the timed Enter source directly and preserve paste plus Option+Backspace behavior. ROOT CAUSE FOUND: send delivered literal text and Enter in one tmux invocation, so a raw-mode TUI read text+CR in a single read() and treated it as a paste (newline in composer) instead of a submit. Fix splits into literal text, then Enter after a 120ms delay so the Enter lands in its own read as a deliberate keypress. Paste and Option+Backspace are separate code paths, untouched.
acceptance: No simultaneous/timed Enter is sent into non-target panes.
Image paste still works in AgentRemote chat and embedded terminal paths.
Option+Backspace / word deletion still works in the Codex terminal path.
Live verification records the exact trigger that previously caused the timed Enter.
tasks.notes: Richard corrected the symptom: this is a simultaneous timed Enter, and the previous guard broke image paste and Option+Backspace. Treat those as required clues, not side effects to ignore. 2026-05-21: fixed via two-phase send (tmux-send-path.js + main.js submitPaneText/sendKeysToCoord), v1.4.15. Integration tests prove two-phase arrives as 2 separate reads (submits) vs combined 1 read (the bug). LIVE-VERIFIED by Richard after HUD relaunch (test send submitted on its own, no manual Enter).

tasks.title: Consolidate task boards into memory taskboard
tasks.description: Collapse the divergent root tasks.json and memory/tasks/tasks.json boards into one canonical taskboard under memory.
acceptance: memory/tasks/tasks.json contains the canonical merged board.
root tasks.json points to the canonical memory board instead of carrying a second task list.
Startup docs point future agents at the canonical board.
tasks.notes: Canonical board moved to memory/tasks/tasks.json; root tasks.json is a pointer; startup/docs references updated.

tasks.title: Find why tmux fallback messages do not send all the way through
tasks.description: Diagnose the AgentRemote/message-agent fallback path where `tmux send-keys` put text into Swarmy's Codex input/queued-message area but did not submit it to the agent. Future work must explain which key sequence or terminal focus/state is required to turn queued text into an actual delivered prompt. ANSWERED: Codex's raw-mode input layer treats literal text and a trailing CR arriving in one read() as a paste (newline in composer), not a submit. Required sequence is text first, THEN Enter as a separate keypress in its own read() — achieved by a delay between the two sends. Same root cause as ALS-LOCAL-006.
acceptance: A failed message-agent send is not reported as delivered.
Tmux fallback proves the message was submitted, not merely queued in the input box.
Delivery evidence includes either recipient action, visible submitted prompt, or an explicit unresolved failure.
tasks.notes: Created after a report to Swarmy failed via message-agent and the tmux fallback visibly landed as queued input instead of being submitted. Do not conflate this with Swarmy's separate launch-runtime bug. 2026-05-21: same fix as ALS-LOCAL-006 (two-phase text+delayed Enter, v1.4.15). Broadcast/composer send (sendKeysToCoord) now resolves only after the Enter actually lands, so 'sent' is not optimistic. LIVE-VERIFIED by Richard after HUD relaunch.

tasks.title: Viewer: native plain-attach ONE iTerm window (BUG B) + close-not-miniaturize
tasks.notes: a150668 (v1.6.1). Switched single-window viewer from iTerm -CC control mode to PLAIN `tmux attach` (one window, plain client) — kills BUG B (deploy opened 2 mini windows). deploy-viewer.js drops 'single' from CONTROL_MODE_LAYOUTS; iterm-attach.js close (not miniaturize). PROVEN live in an isolated throwaway session (exactly one window, plain client, no gateway, no [tmux detached]). Richard then deployed kenpachi live -> one window. BUG A full-close hardening split to ALS-LOCAL-017.

tasks.title: Kenpachi kill/status: identity-leak in launch-agent.sh
tasks.notes: 02930b7. launch-agent.sh only derived MESSAGE_AGENT_IDENTITY when empty (-z), so an agent deployed from inside another agent's session (NEO, env exports MESSAGE_AGENT_IDENTITY=neo-claude) INHERITED the parent identity -> every deployed pane mis-tagged neo-claude -> resolve/illuminate/kill all failed. Fix: always derive identity from AGENT_ID/registry slug, never inherit. Repro test added (fails without fix, passes with). LIVE-CONFIRMED by Richard: deployed kenpachi, menu force-kill worked great.

tasks.title: Composer paste hint + reusable [data-tooltip] hover system
tasks.notes: 5b10afa (v1.6.2). Subtle paste hint above chat ('Cmd-V / Ctrl-V to paste a screenshot') + reusable dark [data-tooltip] hover system (never white native tooltips). renderer-static covers it. NOTE: 21st.dev magic MCP emits React/Tailwind which does not drop into this vanilla single-file HUD; built an equivalent vanilla system. Rollout [data-tooltip] to remaining native title= controls is part of ALS-DESIGN-001.

tasks.title: LIVE CHECK: empty startup_slash means NONE, not default /lead-gogo
tasks.notes: Code complete in 9888d8f (AgentRemote v1.6.3). launch-agent.sh no longer synthesizes /lead-gogo for empty or missing persisted values; AgentRemote scopes /lead-gogo to the new-agent form/create default and preserves explicit empty edits; the renderer copy says empty = none; launch-agent-runtime regression coverage was flipped; audit-lead-startup now treats empty as disabled and resolves the skillvault-owned Codex skill path. Verified locally: red-to-green launcher regression, renderer-static 46/46, Swarmy AgentRemote runtime 36/36, launcher runtime suite pass, syntax/JSON/diff checks pass, lead-startup positive + negative audit fixtures pass. Full npm test reaches 189/190; only the pre-existing unrelated agent-transcript-source:201 test fails. Remaining acceptance: live deploy kenpachi with startup_slash="" and confirm /lead-gogo does not run.

tasks.title: NEXT: BUG A full window-close hardening (iTerm close verb unreliable)
tasks.blocked_by: Richard UX call + live test (electron skills now sourced via ALS-LOCAL-020)
tasks.notes: LRN-20260619-003: iTerm `close` verb no-ops on a window with a running `tmux attach` job (PromptOnQuit=1). The [tmux detached] ghost is already gone (plain attach). For deterministic close on explicit Close: tmux-native teardown -> attach as `tmux attach -t S; exit` (auto-close on session end; note manual Ctrl-b d would also close), or `tmux detach-client` then close. UX call for Richard. Verify LIVE (window count + actual close), never from the green string test alone.

tasks.title: HEADLINE: Elevate AgentRemote into a badass, design-led app
tasks.notes: Richard 2026-06-19: 'doing the bare minimum for improvement instead of just making this a badass app. take a broader approach... completely improve the design and utilize electron skills.' Multi-phase design-elevation pass against DESIGN.md + the $10k website-build quality bar (POV, typography[no Inter], ~5-hex color, hierarchy, imagery, motion, mobile/responsive-by-design, invisible/perf). Scope: (1) visual system + the agent dock/roster, (2) real motion + micro-interactions for deploy/kill/attach/send + hover/press/open-close, (3) electron-native window chrome/behaviors (frameless, vibrancy, traffic-light, multi-display), (4) composer + delivery feedback polish, (5) pet runtime polish + the Codex pet thread, (6) roll [data-tooltip] across all controls. Each phase: brainstorm-first, specialist-led (.claude/agents/tmux-electron-master.md), live-verified, SemVer bump. Do PHASE PLANNING up front (this task or a dispatched Plan agent) before coding.
